name: Sunnah Du'as
epithet: The du'as the Prophet ﷺ carried
title: Sunnah Du'as: the comprehensive personal supplications of the Prophet ﷺ in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: The heart-supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried: for anxiety and grief, after sinning, at wonder and joy, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.
keywords: sunnah dua, dua for anxiety, dua for grief, Allahumma inni abduka, dua after sin, dua for distress, prophetic supplications, comprehensive dua, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Some du'as belong to a time or a place: a morning, a journey, a meal. These do not. These are the supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried inside himself, reached for when grief closed in, when a sin needed washing away, when joy struck and the heart turned at once to its Maker. They are the words of a heart that lived in conversation with Allah.

This is a small gathering of those du'as: the famous plea for the one weighed down by anxiety, the refuge from worry and grief, what to do after you have sinned, and the response of wonder and gratitude when good news arrives. Learn them, and you carry a piece of the Prophet's ﷺ own inner life.
intro: These are not tied to a clock. Reach for number 120, 'O Allah, I am Your slave', when distress and sadness have settled on you; the Prophet ﷺ promised Allah would replace the grief with relief for whoever says it. Number 121 is his standing refuge from worry, debt, and being overpowered. Number 140 is what to do when you have fallen into sin: purify yourself, pray two rak'ahs, and seek forgiveness.

Numbers 240, 241 and 242 are for the other end of the heart, the moment of awe and joy: 'Subhanallah', 'Allahu Akbar', or for great news, a prostration of gratitude to the One who sent it. Recite the Arabic, lean on the transliteration, and read the meaning so the words are truly yours.
closing: What these du'as teach is not a set of phrases but a habit of the heart: in grief you turn to Allah, in sin you turn back to Allah, in wonder you turn toward Allah. The Prophet ﷺ met every state of the soul by carrying it straight to its Lord. Take these few into your chest, and let them be the reflex that meets your own highs and lows, so that whatever the day brings, your heart already knows the way home.

O Allah, make the Qur'an the spring of our hearts and the light of our chests, lift our grief and our worry, accept our return after every fall, and let our first turn in joy and in sorrow always be to You.

faq.q: What is the du'a for anxiety and grief?
faq.a: The most comprehensive is du'a 120, 'Allahumma inni abduka', in which you call upon Allah by every Name He has and ask Him to make the Qur'an the spring of your heart and the remover of your sadness and distress. The Prophet ﷺ said Allah will take away the grief of whoever says it and replace it with relief. Du'a 121 is a shorter, related refuge from worry and grief.

faq.q: What should I do after committing a sin?
faq.a: Follow du'a 140: the Prophet ﷺ taught that any servant who sins, then purifies himself well, prays two rak'ahs, and seeks Allah's forgiveness, will be forgiven. The door of return is never closed; the sunnah is to walk through it at once.

faq.q: Is there a sunnah for moments of joy or good news?
faq.a: Yes. At a sight or moment of wonder the Prophet ﷺ would say 'Subhanallah' (240) or 'Allahu Akbar' (241), and on receiving news that delighted him he would fall into a prostration of gratitude to Allah (242, sajdat ash-shukr). Joy, like sorrow, is met by turning to Him.

email.subject: The du'as the Prophet ﷺ carried inside himself
email.alt_subject: Words for grief, for sin, and for joy
email.preview: The heart-supplications beyond a fixed time or place, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Some du'as belong to a time or a place: a morning, a journey, a meal. These do not. These are the supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried inside himself, reached for when grief closed in, when a sin needed washing away, when joy struck and the heart turned at once to its Maker.

We have gathered a small handful of them, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. The famous plea for the one weighed down by anxiety, the refuge from worry and grief, what to do after you have sinned, and the response of wonder and gratitude when good news arrives.

What they teach is not a set of phrases but a habit of the heart: in grief you turn to Allah, in sin you turn back to Allah, in wonder you turn toward Allah. Carry these few, and your heart will already know the way home, whatever the day brings.

The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
Some du'as belong to a time or a place.

These the Prophet ﷺ carried inside himself.
social.caption:
The du'as the Prophet ﷺ carried.

Not tied to a morning or a journey, these are the heart-supplications he reached for in every state of the soul: the famous plea for the one weighed down by anxiety, the refuge from worry and grief, what to do after you've sinned, and the wonder and gratitude that met good news.

What they teach isn't a set of phrases but a habit of the heart: in grief you turn to Allah, in sin you turn back to Allah, in wonder you turn toward Allah.

Save this, and read or recite the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/sunnah-duas

#SunnahDua #DuaForAnxiety #DuaForGrief #Repentance #PropheticDua #Dhikr #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

120.title: O Allah, I am Your slave
120.note: The great du'a of anxiety and grief. You call on Allah by every Name He has and ask Him to make the Qur'an the spring of your heart and the remover of your sorrow. The Prophet ﷺ said Allah removes the grief of whoever says it and gives relief in its place.

121.title: Refuge from worry and grief
121.note: The Prophet's ﷺ standing refuge from worry and sadness, weakness and laziness, miserliness and cowardice, and from being crushed by debt or overpowered by others.

140.title: After you have sinned
140.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that any servant who sins, then purifies himself well, prays two rak'ahs, and seeks Allah's forgiveness, will be forgiven. The way back, the moment you need it.

240.title: At a sight of wonder: Subhanallah
240.note: 'Glory is to Allah.' Said at a moment of awe, when something draws the heart upward toward its Maker.

241.title: At a moment of awe: Allahu Akbar
241.note: 'Allah is the Most Great.' The Prophet ﷺ would magnify Allah at the sight of what was vast or astonishing.

242.title: The prostration of gratitude
242.note: On receiving news that pleased him, the Prophet ﷺ would fall into prostration in thanks to Allah (sajdat ash-shukr). The body's answer to joy: straight to the ground before the One who sent it.

267.title: Guarding the household at nightfall
267.note: As night falls the Prophet ﷺ taught us to keep the children in, shut the doors, cover the vessels and tie the water-skins, and put out the lamps, mentioning Allah's name over each, for the shaytan cannot open what is closed in His name.
